# Ki Browser

> A private web browser for iPhone. Trackers blocked by default. Per-site control over cookies and scripts. No accounts, no telemetry, no ads.

For comprehensive information, see: [llms-full.txt](https://kibrowser.com/llms-full.txt)

## About

Ki is a privacy-first web browser for iPhone. It blocks cross-site trackers by default and gives the user per-site control over cookies, scripts, and storage. There is no account, no analytics SDK, and no advertising SDK. Bookmarks, history, tabs, and per-site settings stay on the device.

## Key Features

- Cross-site tracker blocking by default (WKContentRuleList)
- Per-site privacy controls in the address-bar shield
- Privacy profiles with isolated cookie jars
- Reader mode
- On-device bookmarks, history, and tabs
- Optional Mac pairing for developers (Web Inspector-style read/run from a paired Mac on the same Wi-Fi network)

## App Details

- **Platform**: iOS 18+
- **Bundle ID**: com.941apps.Ki
- **Developer**: Blake Crosley
- **Website**: https://kibrowser.com
- **App Privacy**: Data Not Collected

## Why "Ki"

Ki (気) is the Japanese word for breath, energy, and presence — the kanji visible on the app icon.

## Contact

- Website: https://kibrowser.com
- Privacy: https://kibrowser.com/privacy
- Support: https://kibrowser.com/support
- Email: blake@941apps.com
